source.page_label: When to Hunt | Kansas Department of Wildlife and Parks
source.update_note: Could not fetch source page. Data from KDWP When to Hunt and eRegulations bag limits.

sub_seasons.name: Youth & Disability Season
sub_seasons.note: Youth (age 15 and under) and disabled hunters; same bag limits as regular season.

licenses.name: Hunting License (Resident, age 16-64)
licenses.covers: General hunting license required for all hunters ages 16-64

licenses.name: Hunting License (Nonresident, age 16+)
licenses.covers: General hunting license required for nonresident hunters

licenses.name: Senior Hunting License (Resident, age 65-74)
licenses.covers: Discounted hunting license for senior residents

key_notes.title: Only cock (male) pheasants may be taken. Kansas has maintained a cocks-only pheasant season since 1982.

sources.label: KDWP — Pheasant Species Page
sources.evidence: Daily bag 4 cocks. Cocks-only format established in 1982. 80-90% of roosters can be safely harvested without hindering reproduction.

sources.label: eRegulations — Kansas Bag Limits
sources.evidence: Pheasant: 4 cocks per day (4x daily limit in possession).

key_notes.title: Youth and Disabled hunters get a 2-day early pheasant and quail season on the first Saturday and Sunday of November.

sources.label: KDWP — When to Hunt
sources.evidence: Pheasant and Quail - Youth and Disability: First Saturday in November and the following day. 2026: November 7-8, 2026.

key_notes.title: Lesser prairie-chicken is CLOSED to hunting in Kansas. Only greater prairie-chicken may be hunted.

sources.label: KDWP — When to Hunt
sources.evidence: Greater Prairie-Chicken: September 15 through January 31 annually. Lesser Prairie-Chicken: Season closed.

key_notes.title: Residents ages 15 and under and ages 75 and older do not need to purchase a hunting license (all other permits, tags, and stamps still apply).

sources.label: KDWP — License & Permit Fees
sources.evidence: Ages 15 and younger are not required to purchase a hunting or fishing license. Ages 75+ are not required to purchase a hunting or fishing license.

key_notes.title: Kansas is among the top pheasant hunting states in the U.S., with an annual harvest of 425,000 to 824,000 cocks.

sources.label: KDWP — Pheasant Species Page
sources.evidence: Kansas is typically among the top 3 or 4 pheasant hunting states in the U.S. Annual harvest since 1990: 425,000 to 824,000 cocks.
